title: RunResult
description: The outcome of running one arm on one scenario at one corpus size. Append-only: a RunResult is never mutated after it is written.

run_id.description: Unique id for this (arm, scenario, seed, model) run.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the run was recorded.

corpus_size_N.description: Number of artifacts in the corpus for this run (drives the adherence-vs-N curve).

temperature.description: null when the pinned model exposes no temperature knob (e.g. Claude Opus 4.8 rejects temperature/top_p/seed). Determinism is then approximated by the fixed model id + scaffold + structured output, and run-to-run variance is reported as a metric.

answering_model.description: The held-constant answering model, pinned identically across arms.

grounding.description: What this arm placed in the answering model's context. The single symmetric grounding opportunity.

embedder.description: Embedder identity for retrieval arms (model id + vector dimension), recorded for reproducibility. null for arms that do not embed.

adherent.description: Headline: did the proposed change respect the governing decision (or correctly invent none)?

stale_decision_followed.description: True if the change followed a superseded decision instead of its successor.

false_permit.description: True if a prohibited action was proposed.

false_prohibit.description: True if a constraint was invented where no decision governs (negative-control failure).

governing_decision_matched.description: True if the cited decision matches the gold governing decision.

governing_decision_retrieved.description: Did this arm's grounding actually contain the gold governing decision? null when no decision governs (negative control). This is the retrieval-recall diagnostic that explains WHY adherence rises or falls — the analog of MemoryBench's Hit@K.
